message:
Fix reverse DNS byte order: use from_ne_bytes instead of from_be_bytes

The reverse_dns() function constructs a sockaddr_in for getnameinfo()
by converting IPv4 octets into a u32 for sin_addr.s_addr. The s_addr
field is defined to be in network byte order, and the octets from
IpAddr are already in network order (big-endian), so the correct
conversion is from_ne_bytes (native-endian pass-through of the raw
bytes) rather than from_be_bytes (which would byte-swap on
little-endian hosts, producing the wrong address).

On little-endian systems (x86_64, aarch64-le), the previous code
would reverse the octets, causing reverse DNS lookups to query the
wrong IP address entirely. This affected both the macOS (sin_family:
u8) and Linux (sin_family: u16) code paths.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
